reference: According to the Behavioral Risk Factor Surveillance System, in Georgia, the percentage of adults aged 18 years and older who have an overweight classification is 39.3% for those with an income of $50,000 - $74,999 and 38.4% for those with an income of $75,000 or greater. In contrast, data from Indiana in 2016 shows that the percentage of adults with an overweight classification is 33.4% for high school graduates, but data is not available for certain demographic groups due to insufficient sample size. These findings suggest that income level and education may be associated with the prevalence of overweight adults, but more data is needed to fully understand these trends.
